State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660        322-5901        322-6441        322-6444

March 2, 1981

Robert A. Kinzie, Jr.
433 Van Ness Avenue
Santa Cruz, CA 95060

Dear Mr. Kinzie:

Thank you for your letter of February 9, 1981, inquiring
as to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act. The facts as I understand them are as follows.

You are a member of the Monterey Bay Unified Air Pollution
Control District. You also own stock in PG&E worth about
$11,000. You asked whether you would ever have to disqualify
yourself from making or participating in decisions affecting
PG&E. Questions of disqualification must always be answered
on a case-by-case basis depending on the nature of the particular
decision to be made. Since you gave no facts, I cannot
advise you in advance as to which decisions would pose a
conflict of interest for you.

The letter you received from your county counsel along
with the relevant excerpts he included from our regulations,
2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18700, et seq., should provide you
with an overview of the standards governing disqualification.
If you have any questions regarding potential conflicts in
specific cases please do not hesitate to call on me.

Sincerely,

Stella Connell Levy
Counsel
Legal Division

MONTEREY COUNTY

OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL

(408) 424-8611, EXT. 296 - P.O. BOX 1987, COURTHOUSE, SALINAS, CALIFORNIA 93902

RALPH R. KUCHLER
COUNTY COUNSEL

February 4, 1981


Robert A. Kinzie, Jr.
433 Van Ness
Santa Cruz, CA 95060

Re: Monterey Bay Unified Air Pollution Control District

Dear Mr. Kinzie:

As I had promised, I am forwarding copies of the California
Administrative standards concerning conflicts of interest rules.
This excerpt comes from Title 2, California Administrative Code,
Section 18700 et seq.; it embodies the legal amplification of
the Political Reform Act of 1974, now codified as Governmental
Code Sections 87100 through 87312.

The essence of the Political Reform Act of 1974 is that a
public official must refrain from making, or even participating
in any governmental decision if that decision will affect the
financial interest of the public official to the extent that the
official is no longer impartial. The Act defines categories of
interests so that the "material financial effect" of a governmental decision is an objective rather than a subjective analysis.

A conflicting interest can arise in a variety of ways. One
instance is where a public official has a direct investment
worth more than $1000, and will make a decision that is likely
to increase or decrease either the net income or gross revenue
of the business by $50,000 or more (or by 0.5 percent of assets
or liabilities), then a conflict of interest is apparent. It is
the public official's responsibility to refrain from all participation in a matter which bears on this conflict, and further,
the official should state the existence of the conflict on the
record.

The requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974 will
not be lessened by your reliance upon the advice of either
District Counsel or privately retain counsel. Should you have a
particular factual question to be resolved, it is possible to
seek an opinion from the Fair Political Practices Commission

Robert A. Kinzie, Jr.                    -2-                    February 4, 1981


(FPPC) as to the existence or non-existence of a conflict as
these rules relate to your circumstance. An FPPC opinion would
act to insulate you from any spectre of wrong doing.

     Naturally, no FPPC opinion would be necessary in a circum-
stance where you proclaim a conflict and refrain from partici-
pation. No inference would result therefrom, and your continued
participation in other hearing Board matters would be entirely
proper. If you choose, you may attend all Board meetings,
including those which affect your interests. It is suggested
that you disclose the fact of any conflict which may exist at
each and every meeting which may affect that interest.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        RALPH R. KUCHLER
                                        County Counsel

                                        [illegible]
                                        David C. Laredo
                                        Deputy County Counsel

Enclosure
cc: Larry D. Odle

AMENDMENT PO ___6___ _12_

LEASE BETWEEN THE CITY
OF REDONDO BEACH AND


PORTOFINO, INC.


1. IDENTIFICATION

THIS AMENDMENT TO LEASE is made, entered into and
effective this __11th__ day of ____July_______, 1977, by
and between the CITY OF REDONDO BEACH, a chartered municipal
corporation ("Lessor"), and _PORTOFINO, INC._________

("Lessee").


2. RECITALS

2.1. Lessor and Lessee have heretofore entered into
a Lease dated __March 13________, 19_61_, relating to
certain property within the vicinity of Redondo Beach King
Harbor, which Lease has, from time to time, been amended.

2.2 Lessor and Lessee desire to further amend and
modify said Lease for the purpose of clarifying and correcting
references in said Lease to the Harbor Review Board.


3. AGREEMENTS

The parties agree as follows:

3.1 Paragraph 3 of Article V of that Lease between
Lessor and Lessee dated ___March 13______, 19_61_, is hereby
deleted and the following provision inserted in lieu thereof:

"3. Approval of Plans. Lessee acknowledges
that it is aware that Lessor intends to maintain
certain architectural and structural controls in
the development of the Harbor and Harbor area.

with a view toward the ultimate development of the
area in conformity to a general plan, for the purpose
of maintaining a pleasing and attractive area,
architecturally and structurally, and also for the
purpose of developing said harbor and harbor area to
the best economical advantage of lessor and all of
the Lessees within the area; that in pursuance of
said plan, all improvements, plans, specifications,
signs and property to be constructed, maintained or
used in said area shall be subject to the approval of
an Architectural and/or Harbor Review Board.

All references in this Lease to "an Architectural and/
or Harbor Review Board" or "said Board" shall mean the
Harbor Review Board of Lessor. Said Board shall con-
sist of seven (7) persons selected by the mayor of
Lessor-City, subject to the approval of the City Council
of said Lessor-City, from among the following:

(a) Two members of the City Council;

(b) Four public members having knowledge or
expertise in the following areas:

• recreational harbors and/or commercial
real estate

• architecture and/or planning;

• business and/or finance

• building and/or engineering

(c) One member from among those individuals or entities
within the Harbor and Pier area having a lease or
sublease with the City.

"All plans, specifications, proposed signs for any and all
proposed leasehold improvements shall first be submitted
to said Board for approval prior to the issuance of any
building permits. The Board, in its actions, shall be

-2-

guided by, and the provisions of this Lease shall be
subject to, the architectural and construction standards
set forth in Exhibit "D", called the Design Criteria,
attached hereto and by reference made a part hereof."

3.2 Except as hereinabove amended, said Lease shall
remain in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the CITY OF REDONDO BEACH has caused
this Amendment to Lease to be executed by its Mayor and
City Clerk, first hereunto duly authorized, and the seal
of said City to be affixed hereto, and Lessee has affixed
its signature hereto, all the day and year first hereinabove
written.

CITY OF REDONDO BEACH, a
chartered municipal corporation,

By [illegible] City Mayor

By [illegible] Deputy City Clerk

"Lessor"

By [illegible]
[illegible]
By [illegible]
[illegible]

"Lessee"

APPROVING AS TO FORM:

[illegible]
City Attorney

6. ORDINANCE NO. 1682.

It is understood and agreed by and between the parties
hereto that this lease is made and executed in full conformity
with the provisions of Lessor's Ordinance No. 1682, as amended
by Ordinances Nos. 1711 and 1815, copies of which are attached
hereto as Exhibit "B".


7. IMPROVEMENTS TO BE CONSTRUCTED BY LESSEE.

7.1 NATURE, PLANNING, APPROVAL AND CONSTRUCTION OF
    IMPROVEMENTS SET FORTH IN OPTION TO LEASE.

Under the terms of that certain Option to Lease
(Exhibit "a"), Lessee is required to plan certain improve-
ments required to be constructed upon a portion of the
Premises. In part consideration for this Lease, Lessee will
cause said improvements to be constructed within the time
provided in the construction schedule submitted with the
plans, designs and drawings required under said Exhibit "B"
to be located on the Premises.


7.2 APPROVAL OF PLANS FOR ADDITIONAL IMPROVEMENTS.

(a) All plans, specifications or other designs
(including environmental impact reports) for any and all
buildings, installations or other improvements proposed by
Lessee for additional construction on the Premises after
the execution of this Lease shall, prior to Lessee's appli-
cation for the issuance of any building permits, first be
submitted to the Director of Property Management of Lessor
for review by the Harbor Review Board.

(b) Such plans, specifications, or other designs
so submitted to the Harbor Review Board shall be reviewed

-17-

by said Board, and said Board shall approve, reject, or
request revisions, modifications, or corrections of the
plans, specifications, or other designs within ten (10)
working days after the date of submission. The action of
said Board shall be forthwith communicated to Lessee in
writing pursuant to the notice provisions act forth in
Article 29 hereof. In the event such plans, specifications,
or other designs are rejected or requested to be modified
by said Board, the reasons for such rejection or required
modification shall also be communicated in writing to
Lessee by said Board in the manner provided. In the event
said Board requests revision, modification, or correction
of the plans, specifications, or other designs so submitted,
Lessee shall have thirty (30) working days from the date
of action by said Board to submit a revised, modified, or
corrected plan, specification, or other design, as the case
may be. In the event such plans, specifications, or other
designs are neither approved nor rejected by said Board
within ten (10) working days after the submission thereof
by Lessee, then such plans, specifications, or other designs
shall be deemed to have been approved. Any approval, whether
actual or deemed, of plans, specifications, or other designs
under this Article 7 shall in no event be considered as
approval of any application for a building permit. Any
provisions in this Lease to the contrary notwithstanding,
Lessee shall secure any and all permits required by the
ordinances of Lessor or by any other government or govern-
mental agency for the construction of any building, installa-
tion, or other improvement on the Premises.

-10-

(c) Said Board, in its review and decision of
any plan, specifications, or other design submitted pursuant
to the terms hereof, shall be guided by the provisions of
this Lease, as well as the architectural and construction
standards set forth in that document entitled "Design
Criteria", a copy of which is attached hereto as Exhibit
"H" and by this reference incorporated herein.

7.3 SCHEDULE OF COMMENCEMENT AND COMPLETION OF
CONSTRUCTION OF IMPROVEMENTS ON THE PREMISES.

(a) On or before thirty (30) days from the date of
the execution of this Lease, Lessee shall submit to the
Harbor Review Board "schematic plans", as that term is
defined and used in the Design Criteria, for the construction
on the Premises of those improvements called for in Article
7.2 above.

(b) On or before sixty (60) days immediately
following the date on which the Harbor Review Board approves
the above-mentioned schematic plans, Lessee shall submit to
the Harbor Review Board "preliminary plans", as that term is
defined and used in the Design Criteria, for the construction
on the Premises of those improvements called for in Article
7.2 above.

(c) On or before ninety (90) days immediately
following the date on which the Harbor Review Board approves
the next above-mentioned preliminary plans, Lessee shall
submit to the Harbor Review Board "working drawings", as
that term is defined and used in the Design Criteria, for
the construction on the Premises of those improvements
called for in Article 7.2 above.

-19-

The facts as I understand them are as follows. You are a member of the Monterey Bay Unified Air Pollution Control District. You also own stock in PG&E worth about $11,000. You asked whether you would ever have to disqualify yourself from making or participating in decisions affecting PG&E. Questions of disqualification must always be answered on a case-by-case basis depending on the nature of the particular decision to be made. Since you gave no facts, I cannot advise you in advance as to which decisions would pose a conflict of interest for you. The letter you received from your county counsel along with the relevant excerpts he included from our regulations, 2 Cal. Adm. Code Sections 18700, et seq., should provide you with an overview of the standards governing disqualification. If you have any questions regarding potential conflicts in specific cases please do not hesitate to call on me. Sincerely, Stella Connell Levy Counsel Legal Division ROBERT A. KINZIE, JR. 433 VAN NESS AVENUE SANTA CRUZ, CALIFORNIA 95060 Gentlemen - I am a member of the Hearing Board of the M.B. U.A.P.C.D. I own P.G & E. stock now worth about $11,000 and a question arose about conflict of interest. Because a hearing board can not waive the enforcement of a regulation but only delay it. And only under very special conditions May it be granted for a year (with continuous progress towards compliance). It is hard to see where any action of ours could cause a decrease of the magnitude mentioned in Mr. Sarek's letter (attached). I would appreciate your opinion on this case, and others like it. Respectfully, Robert C. Kinzie Jr. February 4, 1981 Robert A.
